facade_description: 190 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a dark maroon/brown shuttered storefront with a red house of spice banner sign palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 2 upper-window bays, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: The properties at 219 and 225 Augusta Avenue each have value as a nineteenth-century Houseform building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations.
